Film, Communication, Creative & Performing Arts Institute

[newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Whistling+Woods+International%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Whistling+Woods+International%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Whistling+Woods+International%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Whistling+Woods+International%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(February 2016)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

is a film, communication and creative arts institute located in [Mumbai](/wiki/Mumbai "Mumbai"), [India](/wiki/India "India"). Founded in 2006 by [Subhash Ghai](/wiki/Subhash_Ghai "Subhash Ghai"). The institute is promoted by the Indian Filmmaker [Subhash Ghai](/wiki/Subhash_Ghai "Subhash Ghai"), [Mukta Arts](/wiki/Mukta_Arts "Mukta Arts") and [Film City](/wiki/Film_City,_Mumbai "Film City, Mumbai") Mumbai. In July 2014, *[The Hollywood Reporter](/wiki/The_Hollywood_Reporter "The Hollywood Reporter")* named Whistling Woods International on its list of "The best film schools in the world".[[2]](#cite_note-hollywood2014-2) The school was first ranked among the top 10 film schools by The Hollywood Reporter in 2010.[[1]](#cite_note-hollywood2010-1)
["Study Abroad: The Top 15 International Film Schools"](https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/news/study-top-15-international-film-721648). *The Hollywood Reporter*.

["Whistling Woods International– Transforming Students into Employable and Industry-Ready Professionals"](https://theknowledgereview.com/whistling-woods-international-transforming-students-employable-industry-ready-professionals/). *The Knowledge Review*. 23 November 2018. Retrieved 27 May 2020.

**Whistling Woods International** is a film, communication and creative arts institute located in [Mumbai](/wiki/Mumbai "Mumbai"), [India](/wiki/India "India"). Founded in 2006 by [Subhash Ghai](/wiki/Subhash_Ghai "Subhash Ghai"). The institute is promoted by the Indian Filmmaker [Subhash Ghai](/wiki/Subhash_Ghai "Subhash Ghai"), [Mukta Arts](/wiki/Mukta_Arts "Mukta Arts") and [Film City](/wiki/Film_City,_Mumbai "Film City, Mumbai") Mumbai. In July 2014, *[The Hollywood Reporter](/wiki/The_Hollywood_Reporter "The Hollywood Reporter")* named Whistling Woods International on its list of "The best film schools in the world".[[2]](#cite_note-hollywood2014-2) The school was first ranked among the top 10 film schools by The Hollywood Reporter in 2010.[[1]](#cite_note-hollywood2010-1)

Programmes offered at Whistling Woods International vary in duration from 1 year to 4 years. All the major specialisations of the Media, Communication & Creative industry are catered to in the seven schools housed at Whistling Woods International, Namely, School of Filmmaking, Actors' Studio, School of Animation, School of Design, School of Fashion, School of Media & Communication and School of Music.

Whistling Woods International has affiliated with 1 universities to offer 2, 3 and 4 year graduate and postgraduate programs. They are the [Tata Institute of Social Sciences](/wiki/Tata_Institute_of_Social_Sciences "Tata Institute of Social Sciences")[[3]](#cite_note-theknowledgereview.com-3)
